title: Budget boosts capex with faster infra approvals
text: The Union Budget increases infrastructure capex allocation and accelerates approvals for roads, ports, and urban transit. Analysts expect higher order inflows for engineering firms and construction equipment demand to pick up. Toll, logistics, and public-private partnership pipelines are likely to expand over the next 6–12 months.

title: Renewables get higher incentives for grid storage
text: New incentives expand support for solar and wind projects alongside grid-scale battery storage. The policy emphasizes faster interconnection timelines and streamlined clearances. Developers and equipment suppliers anticipate improved project economics and quicker commissioning.

title: Manufacturing push: production-linked tax credits rise
text: Production-linked tax credits for domestic manufacturing are raised, targeting electronics, specialty chemicals, and capital goods. Companies with eligible capex plans may face lower effective tax rates. The move is seen as a catalyst for incremental manufacturing capacity and supplier ecosystem growth.

title: Capital gains changes spark positioning in midcaps
text: Proposed adjustments to capital gains rules and related disclosures alter investor holding strategies. Traders expect more short-term rebalancing as tax treatment uncertainty clears. Market participants are likely to rotate into sectors perceived as benefiting from reinvestment themes.

title: Healthcare outlay increases for primary care
text: The Budget allocates additional funds toward primary healthcare, diagnostics, and rural workforce training. Procurement and service capacity expansion is expected. Investors watch for gradual revenue uplift for healthcare providers and suppliers.

title: Banking sector: deposit growth incentives announced
text: Deposit mobilization incentives for banks are expanded, alongside measures to improve credit distribution to priority sectors. Credit growth guidance remains intact, but compliance requirements increase. Analysts expect loan growth to remain steady with a shift toward retail and SME segments.

title: Customs duty tweaks for semiconductors and electronics
text: Customs duty rationalization reduces input costs for semiconductor manufacturing and electronics assembly. The change improves margins for downstream assemblers and may attract suppliers. Equipment providers anticipate order normalization as capex plans firm up.

title: Education funding accelerates AI talent programs
text: Education spending includes new AI and analytics training tracks in collaboration with universities and industry. Funding supports scholarships, compute access, and curriculum updates. Tech services demand is expected to benefit from longer-term talent availability.

title: Defense modernization gets multi-year contracts
text: A multi-year defense modernization plan outlines procurement for surveillance, naval upgrades, and unmanned systems. Contract visibility improves order backlog planning. Suppliers anticipate higher utilization and stable cash flows.

title: Road safety and logistics reforms reduce turnaround times
text: Road safety spending and logistics digitization targets lower turnaround times. Digital documentation and monitoring reduce compliance friction for freight. Logistics firms expect improved asset utilization and better working capital discipline.

title: Telecom: spectrum auctions deferred with roll-out conditions
text: Spectrum-related auctions are deferred, but roll-out conditions tighten for 4G/5G coverage. Operators face capex discipline requirements, and compliance reporting increases. Equity reaction is expected to favor players with clear deployment schedules.

title: Agriculture: MSP coverage expands for pulses and oilseeds
text: Minimum support coverage expands for pulses and oilseeds with targeted storage subsidies. The policy aims to reduce volatility and stabilize supply. Traders expect demand for cold-chain infrastructure and processing units to rise.

title: Real estate: stamp duty relief for affordable housing
text: Stamp duty relief is extended for affordable housing projects with faster completion incentives. Developers may see improved sales velocity. Analysts highlight risks around timelines but expect incremental demand in tier-2 markets.

title: IT services: export support continues; compliance streamlined
text: Export-oriented support continues for IT services, alongside streamlined compliance for eligible contracts. Clients may reduce lead times for procurement. Services firms anticipate improved order visibility from global clients.

title: Carbon credit framework clarifies renewable project revenue
text: A clarified carbon credit framework improves bankability for renewable and efficiency projects. Documentation requirements are simplified and verification timelines shorten. Investors expect more stable revenue assumptions for developers.

title: Consumer demand: targeted subsidies lift demand for essentials
text: Targeted subsidies for essentials raise near-term purchasing power among lower- and middle-income households. Retail analysts expect incremental volume growth for packaged foods and household categories. Margins may remain steady due to logistics support.

title: SME credit: guarantee scheme widened; risk weights adjusted
text: A widened credit guarantee scheme encourages lending to SMEs, with adjusted risk weights for eligible collateral. Bankers anticipate faster approvals but continue to demand robust documentation. Equity reaction may favor lenders and credit platforms with stronger SME footprints.

title: Market reaction: yields steady; sectors reprice on capex optimism
text: Government bond yields remain contained as fiscal targets look credible. Equity markets reprice toward capex-sensitive sectors. Analysts caution that implementation speed will determine how long the rally lasts.
